text: Machine learning is a way for computers to learn from data without being explicitly programmed for every task. One common type is supervised learning, where the computer trains on labeled data: for example, photos of cats and dogs with correct names. This training data helps the computer build a model—a kind of internal rule set. Once the model is ready, it can make predictions on new, unseen photos. The model's accuracy shows how often its predictions are correct. By improving the training data and adjusting the model, engineers can make predictions more reliable over time.
